======= RMA00798Cornell University Centennial Celebration records47-4-798Compiled by Sarah E. KeatingDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Centennial Celebration records
Cornell University. Centennial Celebration
47-4-798
12.9 cubic feet. 12.9 cubic feet.
1957-1967,-1960-1965 (bulk) Correspondence, newsclippings, photographs and subject files This collection contains materials relating to Cornell University's Centennial Celebration which took place in 1965. Planning material, correspondence, speeches and photographs all detail the events which took place in celebration of Cornell University's 100th year. Collection material in English 2079976
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Sarah E. Keating
Date completed:
June 2003
EAD encoding:
Sarah E. Keating, June 2003
Date modified:
RMC Staff, October 2013
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records concerning the year-long Cornell University Centennial observance (1964-65) and the related development program, including correspondence and preliminary plans and statements; mimeographed minutes, memoranda, studies, reports And agenda of the Planning Committee, established in 1960; statements of long-range plans and goals submitted to the Committee by the Deans of each of the colleges and schools in Ithaca; reports prepared by the various university departments and divisions for meetings with the Sub-Committees on the Humanities, the Natural Sciences, and the Social Sciences and reports of these sub-committees; papers written by staff and faculty members on university-wide problems; drafts and final copies of Centennial brochures; correspondence, scripts, and memoranda mainly of the Centennial Celebration Committee pertaining to Convocation (October 1964), Cornell Week at Lincoln Center (March 1965), Charter Week (April-May, 1965), the presentation at Cornell of Berlioz's Te Deum by the Philadelphia Orchestra, Alumni Reunion Week culminating in the son et lumière program, written and produced by Donald Mainwaring and other Centennial events; also programs, texts of addresses and symposia and other printed matter, committee minutes, schedules, invitations, photographs, press releases, and tape recordings; congratulatory letters and approximately 120 scrolls and similar documents from colleges, universities and learned societies here and abroad. The chief correspondents are Robert Kidera and John Marcham; other persons represented in the records include Sir Eric Ashby, Morris Bishop, Walter Brusk, Richard H. Comstock, Alfred R. Crawford, Arthur H. Dean, Mary H. Donlon, George Healy, Karel Husa, Ernest Leet, Thomas Mackesey, Deane W. Malott, Otakar Matousek, Janson Noyes, Jrl, Eugene Ormandy, James A. Perkins, Felix Reichmann, Adlai Stevenson and James L. Zwingle.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Record of Delegates, Scrolls and Greetings Received During the Centennial Year
Boxes 1-2
Series II. Complete Set of Speeches at All Major Events of the Centennial Year
Box 3
Series III. Centennial Correspondence, Publications and Planning Material
Boxes 4-8
Series IV. Cornell Centennial Planning Committee
Boxes 9-10
Series V. President's Office
Boxes 11-13
Series VII. Collection #47-4-1474
Boxes 1-3
Series VIII. Memorabilia
Box 14
Cite As:
Cornell University Centennial Celebration Records, #47-4-798. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Son et lumière. Lighting -- Special effects. Ashby, Eric, 1904-1992. Bishop, Morris, 1893-1973. Bruska, Walter. Comstock, Richard H. Crawford, Alfred R. Dean, Arthur H. Donlon, Mary H., 1893-1977. Healy, George. Husa, Karel, 1921-2016. Kidera, Robert. Leet, Ernest D. Mackesey, Thomas W. (Thomas William), 1908-1976. Mainwaring, Donald. Malott, Deane W. (Deane Waldo), 1898-1996. Marcham, John, 1927- Matoušek, Otakar. Noyes, Janson, Jr. Ormandy, Eugene, 1899- Perkins, James Alfred, 1911-1998. Reichmann, Felix, 1899-1987. Stevenson, Adlai E., III (Adlai Ewing), 1930- Zwingle, James L. Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. Cornell University -- : Anniversaries, etc. Cornell University -- : Pictorial works. Cornell University -- : History. Cornell University -- : Planning.

MemorabiliaSix wine glasses produced by the Owens-Illinois Glass Company, Ohio at the request of Preston Levis '24. The mold was destroyed by request, after only one use.14 ======= Series I.
